fkcompute
  • fkcompute documentation
  • Edit on GitHub

fkcompute documentation

fkcompute is a Python package and command-line tool for computing the FK invariant \(F_K(x, q)\) of knots and links presented as braid closures. It combines a Python orchestration layer with a compiled C++ backend for high-performance polynomial computation.

from fkcompute import fk

# Compute F_K for the trefoil knot at degree 2
result = fk([1, 1, 1], 2)
print(result["metadata"]["components"])  # 1

# Equivalent CLI invocation
fk simple "[1,1,1]" 2
Installation

System dependencies, pip install, optional extras, and Gurobi setup.

Installation

Get up and running with your first FK computation in minutes.

What the FK invariant is, where it comes from, and how this package computes it.
